name: Carla Bley
description: American jazz composer, pianist, organist and bandleader (1936–2023)
abstract: Carla Bley (born Lovella May Borg; May 11, 1936 – October 17, 2023) was an American jazz composer, pianist, organist, and bandleader. An important figure in the free jazz movement of the 1960s, she gained acclaim for her jazz opera Escalator over the Hill (released as a triple LP set), as well as a book of compositions that have been per…

interviews.title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: Oregon, with Sun Ra, Piano, and the Carla Bley Band

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Stewart
about_quotes.evidence: Carla Bley and Mike Mantler were doing things and they invited me to do tuba on some of their things. I actually sang on one of their — “Escalator Over the Hill,” that, one of those early JCOA albums. And then I played on some of her other things and that’s when I was in her band
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Stewart interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 24, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve watched quite a few of my friends and co-performers, collaborators—Carla Bley, Chick Corea, Keith Jarrett and so on—be given this honor in recent years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Arturo O'Farrill
about_quotes.evidence: this I learned by the way, again, from Carla Bley. Carla Bley wrote for her musicians. I saw it up close. She wrote for Gary Valente, she wrote for me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Arturo O'Farrill interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 6, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: But at this jam session was Roland Kirk, Carla Bley, who started using the tuba for everything shortly after that, Paul Bley was there too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Kernfeld
about_quotes.evidence: Steve Swallow told me is that they went around and they talked to the Berklee guys who got their tunes in... Carla Bley, and so forth
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Kernfeld interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 16, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Davis
about_quotes.evidence: And you hear these great songwriters talk about it from Billy Joel to Sting to Carla Bley, you know all range of styles.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2024
